target: data/wins.json -> WIN id="067". Five field-level edits in WIN-067 to (a) disclose the linear-coupling assumption behind 1,430× in finding/tldr_evidence/tldr_verdict, (b) reframe the g↔H(r) link in detail_evidence as an IF-THEN conditional grounded in dome raw-text quotations rather than asserting a coupling the dome never explicitly states, and (c) drop the geographically incorrect 'Antarctic-interior' label on the 9.826 m/s² figure in detail_extra paragraph (ii) and tldr_evidence. No other WIN-067 fields are modified. No other WIN is modified.
patch_instructions: Patch 1 — finding (ISS-1356). In data/wins.json, locate WIN id="067". Replace the entire `finding` field value with the string in `replacement_finding` below. Old length 167 chars; new length ~290 chars.

Patch 2 — tldr_evidence (ISS-1356 + ISS-1355). Replace the entire `tldr_evidence` field value with the string in `replacement_tldr_evidence` below. The new TLDR (a) discloses linear coupling for the headline 1,430× while preserving the lower-bound 170×, and (b) corrects '80°S' to '~70°S (Antarctic coast/Southern Ocean)' to match the WGS84 normal-gravity latitude that produces 9.826 m/s².

Patch 3 — tldr_verdict (ISS-1356). Replace the entire `tldr_verdict` field value with the string in `replacement_tldr_verdict` below. Adds the 'under linear coupling' qualifier so the verdict TLDR is consistent with the new evidence TLDR.

Patch 4 — detail_evidence (ISS-1357). In WIN-067 `detail_evidence`, find the sentence 'The dome model\'s stated physics links gravity to the aetheric pressure gradient driven by column height H(r).' and the sentence that follows it ('Under any mechanism where gravitational effects depend monotonically on H(r) — linearly, as a power law, or otherwise — the 10× difference in H(r) between pole and rim must translate to substantially different gravity.'). REPLACE BOTH SENTENCES (verbatim, no surrounding whitespace changes) with the exact string in `replacement_detail_evidence_ifthen` below. Do NOT modify the preceding H(r) arithmetic sentence ('The dome model\'s height function H(r)... = 837 km.') and do NOT modify the following sentence beginning 'Under linear scaling: g_rim/g_pole = 837/8,537 = ...'. The replacement preserves the chain of inference but routes it through an explicit IF-THEN built on dome raw-text quotations.

Patch 5 — detail_extra paragraph (ii) (ISS-1355). In WIN-067 `detail_extra`, find the substring 'The observed Antarctic-interior gravity — the Antarctic-latitude gravity value cited in the objection (~9.826 m/s²) —' and replace with 'The observed Antarctic-latitude gravity at the value cited in the objection — ~9.826 m/s², which the WGS84 Somigliana formula places at ~70°S (Antarctic coast / Southern Ocean transition; true interior 80–90°S sits at 9.8306–9.8322 m/s², so this anchoring is geographically conservative and the ratio rises to ~5,600× if interior gravity is used instead) —'. This single-string substitution is the only change to detail_extra; do not touch paragraphs (i), (iii), or 'Net effect.', and do not change the ≈ 1,430× headline that follows.
replacement_finding: Dome's own H(r) implies ≥38% gravity drop at the Antarctic rim under any monotonic g↔H(r) coupling: 1,430× apples-to-apples error under linear coupling, ~590× under the most-forgiving H^(1/5) scaling, with 170× as a dimensionless lower bound from the whole-globe pole-equator spread.
replacement_tldr_evidence: The dome's own height function H(r) collapses by a factor of ten between the pole and the Antarctic rim, so any monotonic coupling of gravity to column height predicts a large gravity drop there — 90% under linear coupling, ~38% even under the most-forgiving H^(1/5) scaling. At ~70°S (the Antarctic coast / Southern Ocean transition latitude where the WGS84 Somigliana formula gives 9.826 m/s²) — a deliberately conservative anchor against the dome defender's own choice — measured gravity is just 0.006 m/s² (0.063%) below the polar reference. Dividing the dome's predicted 8.87 m/s² drop (linear coupling) by this 0.006 m/s² measured drop gives 1,430×; ~590× under H^(1/5); 170× from the dimensionless whole-globe spread; ~5,600× if anchored to true interior (80°S, 9.8306 m/s²). The dome invokes an undefined 'Sub-terrestrial structure' correlation (Domain 19 of live-power page) with no published mechanism or equations.
replacement_tldr_verdict: The dome's own height formula predicts 90% gravity loss at the Antarctic rim under linear g↔H(r) coupling (38% even under the gentlest H^(1/5) scaling). Comparing to the actual Antarctic-latitude measurement gives a 1,430× error under linear coupling; ~590× under H^(1/5); 170× as a conservative dimensionless lower bound from the whole-globe spread. The 'Sub-terrestrial structure' escape hatch has no equations and no measurements.
replacement_detail_evidence_ifthen: The dome's stated narrative ties gravity to aetheric density variation — Domain 4 names the mechanism “Dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation” (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 69) — and ties aetheric pressure to disc geometry: “The disc edge creates an aetheric pressure gradient. That gradient drives both the anomalous geomagnetic field minimum (SAA) and the anomalous atmospheric pressure pattern (Roaring 40s)” (raw-text/02-wins.txt line 31). The dome does not, as of V51.2, publish a sentence that explicitly says g = f(H(r)). It does, however, choose its only quantitative gravity formula's exponential decay length λg = 8,619 km to be identical to the H(r) decay constant 8,619 km (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 70 vs. raw-text/05-model.txt line 16) — a length-scale identity that is otherwise unexplained. The argument that follows therefore proceeds as a conditional rather than an attribution: IF the dome's narrative is taken at face value — aetheric pressure depends on column height H(r), gravity depends on aetheric density, and the shared 8,619 km length scale is meaningful — THEN gravitational effects must depend monotonically on H(r), and the 10× difference in H(r) between pole and rim must translate to substantially different gravity. The only escape is to deny the conditional — to say the dome's only quantitative gravity formula (Domain 4, treated below) is causally independent of H(r) despite sharing its decay constant — in which case the dome has no dome-specific gravity mechanism at all, only a WGS84-clone reskin (the second self-contradiction documented after the H(r) chain).
word_count_delta: +472 words across five fields; net field totals: finding 24→47, tldr_evidence 79→153, tldr_verdict 50→71, detail_evidence ~793→~960, detail_extra ~600→~644.
holes_addressed: ISS-1356 / WIN-067.c6 H3 (moderate): finding now discloses the linear-coupling assumption behind the 1,430× headline, names ~590× as the apples-to-apples ratio under the most-forgiving H^(1/5) scaling, and preserves 170× as a dimensionless lower bound. The defender's 'attacking a coupling I never committed to' move is closed at the surface text — every plausible monotonic coupling is now bracketed (linear → 1,430×; H^(1/5) → ~590×; ≥38% drop floor). tldr_evidence and tldr_verdict are updated to match so the disclosure travels with every reading depth.

ISS-1357 / WIN-067.c6 H4 (moderate): detail_evidence is reframed from an asserted attribution ('the dome model's stated physics links gravity to ... H(r)') to an explicit IF-THEN conditional grounded in two verbatim dome raw-text quotations (live-power line 69 'Dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation' as the gravity mechanism, wins.txt line 31 'disc edge creates an aetheric pressure gradient' as the column-height linkage) plus a structural argument from the shared 8,619 km decay constant between Domain 4's λ<sub>g</sub> and H(r). The dome can no longer escape by saying 'I never said gravity depends on H(r)' — the antecedents are quoted verbatim from the live-power and wins pages, and the 8,619-km length-scale identity is on the dome's own scoreboard. The dilemma is also made explicit: deny the IF-THEN and the dome has no dome-specific gravity mechanism at all, only the WGS84-clone Domain 4 formula (the second self-contradiction the WIN already documents).

ISS-1369 / WIN-067.c6 advocate (defense_survives=4): the c6 best_defense was 'your headline says 1,430× and your detail body says 170× — pick one' AND 'the 1,430× presupposes my linear coupling I never published'. Patches 1–4 close both moves: the inconsistency was patched in commit db60a9c (1,430× now leads in finding/tldr/tldr_verdict), and Patches 1–3 of this EXP propagate the disclosure that the headline assumes linear coupling, with H^(1/5) and the 170× dimensionless ratio as bracketing alternatives so the reader sees the full envelope. Patch 4 attaches the antecedents the c6 hole 4 reviewer asked for. After integration, defense_survives should drop to 1–2 (the only residue is the inherently unfalsifiable retreat 'gravity has no dome-specific equation at all', which is itself a concession the WIN already names as the second self-contradiction).

ISS-1355 / WIN-067.c6 H2 (moderate): the geographically incorrect 'Antarctic-interior' label on 9.826 m/s² is removed both from detail_extra paragraph (ii) (Patch 5) and from tldr_evidence (Patch 2). The replacement text follows curmudgeon Option (a) — make explicit that 9.826 m/s² is the WGS84 normal-gravity value at ~70°S, frame the choice as a deliberate concession to the dome defender's own latitude reference, and disclose in-line that re-anchoring to true interior (80°S = 9.8306 m/s²) raises the ratio to ~5,600×. This pre-empts the 'you used a coastal value while saying interior' cherry-pick attack while preserving the 1,430× headline.
new_evidence_added: raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 69 (Domain 4 ECM mechanism field): 'Dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation' — quoted verbatim as the dome's stated gravity mechanism, with link to live.html.

raw-text/02-wins.txt line 31 (WIN-014 / SAA + Roaring 40s narrative): 'The disc edge creates an aetheric pressure gradient. That gradient drives both the anomalous geomagnetic field minimum (SAA) and the anomalous atmospheric pressure pattern (Roaring 40s)' — quoted verbatim as the dome's pressure-from-geometry linkage, with link to wins.html.

Length-scale identity λ_g = 8,619 km (Domain 4 formula in raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 70) = H(r) exponential decay constant 8,619 km (raw-text/05-model.txt line 16) — surfaced in the IF-THEN as a structural inference the dome has not explained any other way.

WGS84 Somigliana-formula latitude check confirming 9.826 m/s² ≈ 70°S (not interior 80–90°S, where 9.8306–9.8322 m/s²) — used in tldr_evidence and detail_extra to anchor the 'conservative' framing without ceding the 1,430× headline.

H^(1/5) coupling ratio ~590× (verified by the c6 reviewer: g_pred_rim = 9.8322 × (837/8537)^0.2 = 6.179 m/s², drop = 3.65 m/s², 3.65/0.0062 ≈ 589) — promoted from detail_evidence body into surface texts so the most-forgiving plausible coupling is on the headline.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'Your IF-THEN is rhetorical sleight-of-hand — you quoted two sentences that don\'t mention gravity at all ("aetheric density variation" is the Domain 4 mechanism label, and the wins.txt quote is about SAA + Roaring 40s, not gravity), then you stitched them together with "and gravity depends on aetheric density" as if I wrote it. I never wrote it.'
anticipated_objections.response: The IF-THEN is explicitly conditional, not attributive: the new text says 'the dome\'s narrative ties gravity to aetheric density variation' citing Domain 4\'s own ECM-mechanism field verbatim ('Dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation'), and 'ties aetheric pressure to disc geometry' citing wins.txt verbatim. The conjunction of those two dome statements yields g = f(aetheric density) AND aetheric density = f(disc geometry, including H(r)) by transitivity. If the defender denies the transitivity — i.e., insists the gravity formula's 'aetheric density variation' is causally unrelated to the wins.txt 'aetheric pressure gradient' that drives SAA and Roaring 40s — then the dome has two unrelated 'aetheric' mechanisms with the same name and no published reason to share notation. Either the two are linked (in which case the IF-THEN is the dome's own structure, not the review's invention) or they are not (in which case the dome has chosen to name two unrelated mechanisms identically, which is a separate problem the review can flag in a follow-up). The shared 8,619 km decay constant between Domain 4's λ_g and H(r) is the third leg making accidental name-collision implausible.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'The shared 8,619 km decay length is a coincidence of the formula-fitting process — I matched my exponential to the empirical Antarctic gravity profile, which independently happens to share a length scale with the firmament curve. Length-scale identity is not mechanism identity.'
anticipated_objections.response: Then the dome owes a derivation of λ_g = 8,619 km from independent empirical data — specifically, from a fit that does not invoke the H(r) curve constants. No such derivation appears in raw-text/08-live-power.txt; Domain 4 simply prints the formula. If the value were independently fit to gravity-only data, the dome would presumably say so (the live-power page is otherwise meticulous about labeling 'Source: WGS84 standard' on calibration constants). The far simpler reading is that λ_g = H(r) decay length by construction, which is what the IF-THEN takes as a starting premise. The defender is welcome to publish the independent derivation; until then the length-scale match is evidence of intended coupling, not coincidence.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'You\'ve narrowed the headline from 1,430× to a range (170×–5,600×). That is a softening of the critique. The original 1,430× was cleaner.'
anticipated_objections.response: The range is the honest read; the previous single-number headline was vulnerable to the c6 advocate\'s 'pick one' attack. Disclosure-as-strengthening is the standard pattern for this review: every multi-coupling envelope (588× to 5,593× across linear, H^(1/5), and interior-anchored values) lands the same verdict (Self-Contradicted) and the dome has no monotonic coupling that produces a ratio less than ~590×. A defender who cites the lowest of the range (170× as a dimensionless lower bound, or ~590× under H^(1/5)) is still conceding two-to-three orders of magnitude error and the verdict survives. The headline 1,430× remains in surface text as the apples-to-apples linear-coupling number; the new framing surrounds it with the bracketing context that makes the lower-bound concession unhelpful to the defender.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'If I deny the IF-THEN, you concede I have no dome-specific gravity mechanism — but Domain 4 IS my gravity mechanism, and it predicts 0.476% variation across the rim, well within observation. Your forced concession is bogus.'
anticipated_objections.response: Domain 4 predicts 0.476% gravity variation at the rim — which does match observation, but only because Domain 4 is the WGS84 normal-gravity formula in dome notation (constants 9.7803 and 0.005307 are WGS84 equatorial gravity and gravity flattening verbatim). And Domain 4\'s own falsification text — 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard' (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71) — classifies Domain 4 as FAILED at the rim because the formula's 0.476% rim error exceeds the dome\'s own 0.1% threshold by 4.8×. So the second-horn position the defender takes here is not actually safe ground: the dome has a quantitative gravity formula that (a) is WGS84 in disguise and (b) violates its own falsification rule at the only other geometrically-motivated evaluation point. The text already documents this in the same WIN; the IF-THEN structure simply makes the dilemma's left horn explicit and the right horn unavoidable.

kernel_of_truth: The dome's choice to set the Domain 4 gravity formula's exponential decay length λ_g equal to the H(r) firmament decay constant (both 8,619 km) is a real, deliberate-looking numerical coincidence. The dome author appears to have intended a coupling between gravity and column height — even if no sentence in V51.2 explicitly states it — and the IF-THEN reframing acknowledges this design intent rather than treating the linkage as the review's invention. Likewise, the dome correctly identifies the Antarctic gravity low and its spatial correlation with the SAA boundary (Domain 19 r=0.92) as a real geophysical feature with a shared deep-mantle origin. Both are kernels worth crediting before the critique lands.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: Intending a coupling without publishing one is the dome's structural problem, not its escape route. The IF-THEN now binds the dome to the consequence of its own narrative: any monotonic g↔H(r) coupling consistent with the published 'aetheric density variation' mechanism gives ≥38% rim gravity drop, and the apples-to-apples ratio is at minimum ~590× even under the gentlest H^(1/5) scaling. The only way out is to disown the coupling, which leaves Domain 4 — a WGS84-clone that violates the dome's own 0.1%-error falsification text at the rim by 4.8× — as the dome's only quantitative gravity statement. Either horn convicts the model. The kernel is real but does not produce a third option.

WIN-066 (SAA-related) — shares the dome's claim about Antarctic regional anomalies; the standard-physics mantle-plume explanation cited in WIN-067 is the same explanation that downgrades WIN-066.

Domain 4 self-falsification (raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 71, 'Model error >0.1% vs WGS84 standard') — already in detail_extra paragraph (iii); the IF-THEN block now makes explicit that this self-falsification is the right horn of the dilemma the dome cannot escape after disowning the H(r) coupling.

unaddressed_holes_note: c6 review lists five holes. EXP-215 addresses H2 (geographic mislabel, ISS-1355 — opportunistically batched), H3 (linear-coupling disclosure, ISS-1356), and H4 (dome citation / IF-THEN, ISS-1357). H1 (1,430× vs 170× internal inconsistency) was patched in commit db60a9c per the c6 trigger field; this EXP does not re-touch the field but Patches 1–3 ensure the new framing in finding / tldr_evidence / tldr_verdict is internally consistent with the existing 1,430×-led detail_extra paragraph (ii). H5 (minor: replace Shepherd 2012 with Whitehouse 2012 / Ivins 2013 for GIA) is out of EXP-215 scope — it is not in the issue list (no ISS- assigned to H5) and the current Shepherd citation is loose-but-not-wrong. Recommend the decider either fold H5 into a follow-up curmudgeon-side citation patch or open it as ISS-NEW for the next analyst run.

research_notes.dome_source_verification: Verified raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 69 ('Dielectric pressure with aetheric density variation' as Domain 4 ECM mechanism) and raw-text/02-wins.txt line 31 ('disc edge creates an aetheric pressure gradient...drives both...SAA...and...Roaring 40s') in the snapshot tree this run via grep. Confirmed there is NO sentence anywhere in raw-text/01-home through raw-text/08-live-power that explicitly states g = f(H(r)) — searched for 'gravit', 'H(r)', 'column height', 'aether.*pressure', 'dielectric'. Closest evidence is the shared 8,619 km decay constant between Domain 4's λ_g and H(r), used in the IF-THEN as the structural-inference leg.
research_notes.geographic_anchor: WGS84 Somigliana formula gives g(latitude) = (g_a·cos²φ + (b/a)·g_b·sin²φ) / √(cos²φ + (b/a)²·sin²φ). At φ = 70°S, g ≈ 9.826 m/s² (Antarctic coast / Southern Ocean transition); at φ = 80°S, g ≈ 9.8306; at φ = 90°S, g = 9.8322 (polar reference). The 9.826 figure cited in the original detail_extra paragraph (ii) corresponds geographically to coastal/Southern-Ocean Antarctica, not interior — Patches 2 and 5 surface this explicitly without ceding the headline ratio.
research_notes.advocate_defense_status_after_patch: c6 advocate's best_defense was the 'pick one (170× vs 1,430×) AND I never published linear coupling' two-pronged attack. Patches in commit db60a9c closed the 'pick one' prong (1,430× now leads). Patches 1–4 of this EXP close the 'never published linear coupling' prong by (a) disclosing the assumption in the headline, (b) bracketing with the H^(1/5) lower bound, and (c) deriving the IF-THEN from raw-text quotations. The only residual move available to the defender is to deny the IF-THEN entirely, which collapses into the right horn (Domain 4 self-falsification by 4.8×) — already covered in detail_extra paragraph (iii).
